question: I have a rule that triggers when a mortal wound is allocated to a model, when does this happen?
answer: When you select a model in the Select Model step of Mortal Wounds (06.02).

question: Does my model or unit become engaged while moving through a enemy units engagement range during a move?

If my unit has weapons that are identical in characteristics, but some of those weapons have [PRECISION] and those weapons targeted a unit without the character keyword, do I roll all of those weapons together?
answer: Yes, the [PRECISION] ability is not an applicable ability for those attacks as there is no CHARACTER in the target unit for the ability to trigger its rule.

question: If a rule says a unit can embark into a Transport in a turn that unit disembarked, does this ignore the core rules restriction on embarking in a turn that unit made a set up move (see 18.02)?
When a rule instructs you to perform a charge outside of the charge phase, do I resolve step 2 and 3 of the Charge step(11.02)?
If a rule modifies how far a unit or model can move during a pile-in move or consolidation move does it also change the distance of the target units?
How many mortals do you take from Hazardous tests for a combined vehicle/monster and Infantry unit
answer: The roll would be all at once and it would be 1 MW per fail

question: My rule says that "this units ranged attacks have +1 A", do each of my attacks get an extra attack dice?
answer: No, it increases the A characteristics of the unit's ranged weapons by 1.

question: If a rule allows my unit to pile-in or consolidate an extra distance in the Fight phase, when can I use that rule?
answer: For pile-in moves, during the Pile-in step(12.02), and for consolidation moves, during the Consolidate step(12.07) of the Fight phase.

question: Can I select a unit to shoot or fight if it doesn't have melee or ranged weapons?
answer: Yes, it just makes no attacks and then the fight type or shoot type is resolved.

question: When checking coherency for my unit can my unit be in multiple groups?
answer: No, it must be in a single contiguous group as per Units and Models (01.02) and What is Coherency in the core rules.

question: When a move says that a model or unit must move in a way if it is able, can that requirement force the unit to end their move not in coherency?
answer: No, when a model or unit is making a move it must follow requirements while ending that move in coherency

question: If the [ANTI] ability on my unit is ANTI-non-Monster/Vehicle does it mean the [ANTI] rule triggers when targeting units that do not have the Monster or Vehicle keyword?
answer: Yes, ANTI-non-(any keyword) will trigger on any unit that does not have the specified keyword.

question: If a unit becomes battle-shocked after starting an action does it stop performing that action?
Aerialists ability allows you to get into a transport even though they disembarked a transport this turn?
answer: Yes, as the unit embarking is not making a normal, advance or fall-back move as part of this embarkation.

question: The Munitorium Field Manual shows my unit can attach to different units then are on it's datasheet, which do I use?
I failed hazard rolls from [Hazardous] weapons, do those mortal wounds have to be allocated to the models with the [Hazardous] weapons?
answer: No, you allocate the mortal wounds to the unit as per Mortal Wounds(06.02). (This represents Bob the guardsmen picking up his fallen squad mates plasma gun or that the twisting power of James the Chaos Sorcerer pulling his followers into the warp.)

question: Can a model be within range of a weapon if it is not visible
When declaring the allocation order of groups in my unit I have more than one non-CHARACTER groups that contain a model with a missing wound, how must I select the order?
answer: Those groups can be selected in an order of your choosing but must be in an order that makes them come before any non-CHARACTER groups that do not contain a model with a missing wound.

question: What does the X" in Lone Operative X" mean?
answer: The X is a placeholder for a number of inches that the ability might appear in a rule with, like Lone Operative 15" where the X has been replaced with 15"

question: If a rule says a unit auto passes Desperate Escape tests does that mean it would auto pass the hazard rolls from a desperate escape move?
If a unit has a rule that allows it to start an action when it is battle-shocked can it also continue doing that action if it becomes battle-shocked after starting an action?
When selecting a target unit can the enemy model that is visible to my model and the enemy model that is in range of my model's weapon be different enemy models in the same unit?
I have a unit being attacked with weapons that will modify some of their save rolls to be worse then 6 should I make those save rolls?
answer: Yes, because other models in your unit may have Sv's that are better those models you've mentioned, this means that enough save rolls could be allocated to the those models that the rest of the results are not greater then the Sv's of the other models in your unit.

question: If a rule says my unit must make a hazard roll does every model in the unit make a hazard roll?
answer: No, the unit would make 1 single hazard roll.

question: Do models destroyed when a unit is out of coherency count for mission objectives?
If my unit has taken control of an objective at the end of my movement phase and that unit has as an ability that secures an objective they control, can they secure that objective?
If my unit has the Deadly Demise ability and has an ability that allows it to Fight on Death, how do I resolve this?
answer: Fight on Death rules are resolved before Deadly Demise abilities, meaning that your unit's Deadly Demise will resolve after your unit has fought/at the end of the phase and the relevant models have been removed from the battlefield.

question: If I have a rule that says my unit can move up to 6" when it makes a pile-in move or consolidation move, does this also mean it changes the maximum distance of those moves to 6"?
If a unit makes any disembark move in a turn (i.e. Disembark move, Emergency Disembark move etc.) has it disembarked that turn?

errata_text:

‘If your Army Faction is **GENESTEALER CULTS**, you start the battle with a number of Resurgence points, depending on the battle size, as shown below.

■ **Incursion**: 6 Resurgence points

Each time a unit from your army is destroyed, if every model in that unit has this ability, you can spend the relevant number of Resurgence points shown below based on that unit’s Starting Strength.

**Aberrants**
5 models 4 Resurgence points
10 models 8 Resurgence points

**Acolyte Hybrids with Autopistols, Acolyte Hybrids with Hand Flamers, Hybrid Metamorphs**
5 models 2 Resurgence points

If you do:
■ Add a new unit to your army identical to your destroyed unit, in Cult Ambush, at its Starting Strength, with all of its wounds remaining and any **[ONE SHOT]** weapons those models are equipped with considered as not having been shot.
■ Place one Cult Ambush marker (see below) anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9" horizontally away from all enemy units (if this is not possible, no marker is placed).

**UNITS IN CULT AMBUSH**
Cult Ambush is a type of Strategic Reserves. Units in Cult Ambush cannot be targeted with the Rapid Ingress Stratagem, but can be set up on the battlefield using a Cult Ambush marker as described below, or by following the rules for Strategic Reserves in a subsequent turn. Units in Cult Ambush are not automically destroyed at the end of the third battle round.

**CULT AMBUSH MARKERS**
Use a circular 32mm diameter marker for Cult Ambush markers. If an enemy model (excluding **AIRCRAFT**) ends any kind of move within 8" of a Cult Ambush marker you placed, that Cult Ambush marker is removed from the battlefield. At the end of your opponent’s next Movement phase, for each of your Cult Ambush markers still on the battlefield, you can select one unit from your army that is in Cult Ambush to make an **ingress move**. For that **ingress move**, set that unit up with at least one model in that unit in base contact with that Cult Ambush marker and all other models placed wholly within 3" of that Cult Ambush marker (that Cult Ambush marker is then removed from the battlefield). Units in Cult Ambush can make an **ingress move** in the first battle round using this rule.'

errata_header: 18.05 - Change to Emergency Disembark Move
errata_text:
WHILE MOVING: Set up each model in your unit:

- Wholly within the set-up distance of that TRANSPORT, and as close as possible to that TRANSPORT.

- Or: If the above is not possible while remaining unengaged, set up that model wholly within the set-up distance of that TRANSPORT, as close as possible to that TRANSPORT, and engaged.

Each model that still cannot be set up is destroyed.

errata_header: Army Rule, Cabal of Sorcerers
errata_text:
Change first paragraph to:

‘If your Army Faction is **THOUSAND SONS**, at the start of your Shooting phase, one or more models from your army with this ability can attempt Rituals from those listed on the right. To do so, select one model from your army with this ability that has not yet attempted a Ritual this turn and select one Ritual no model from your army has attempted to manifest this turn, then take a Psychic test for that model by following the sequence below.’

**Destiny’s Ruin (Psychic)**
Change to:

‘Select one enemy unit within 24” of and visible to the manifesting model. Until the end of the phase, each time a **THOUSAND SONS** or **SCINTILLATING LEGIONS** model from your army makes an attack that targets that unit, re-roll a Hit roll of 1. If the Psychic test result for this Ritual was 10+, you can re-roll the Hit roll instead.’

**Temporal Surge (Psychic)**
Change to:

‘Select one friendly **THOUSAND SONS** or **SCINTILLATING LEGIONS** unit that is not within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units and is within 24” of and visible to the manifesting model. That unit can make a Normal move of up to D6”. If the Psychic test result for this Ritual was 10+, that unit can make a Normal move of up to 6” instead. In either case, until the end of the turn, that unit is not eligible to declare a charge.’

**Twist of Fate (Psychic)**
Change to:

‘Select one enemy unit within 24” of and visible to the manifesting model. Until the end of the phase, each time a **THOUSAND SONS** or **SCINTILLATING LEGIONS** model from your army makes an attack that targets that unit, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1. If the Psychic test result for this Ritual was 12+, improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 2 instead.’

question: Can I use the Summoned by Slaughter Stratagem to deploy Bloodletters in the first battle round?

If I target a unit with the Auto-divinatory Targeting Stratagem while the Protector Imperative is active for my army, in what order are the Ballistic Skill modifiers applied?
answer: The Ballistic Skill characteristic of ranged weapons equipped by models in that unit is first changed to 3+ by the Stratagem, then improved by 1 by the Protector Imperative, for a final result of 2+ (assuming no other modifiers apply).

question: Which Detachments are considered to be *Codex: Space Marines* Detachments?
answer: Every Detachment printed in *Codex: Space Marines* and every Detachment included in the Space Marines Faction Pack.

question: While using the Gladius Task Force Detachment, does a Combat Doctrine need to be active for my army in order to use the Adaptive Strategy Stratagem?
If a Hellblaster is destroyed by anything other than an attack or Hazardous test, can that model use its For the Chapter! ability?
Can I use a Captain’s Rites of Battle ability to target that Captain’s unit if it is in Reserves or Strategic Reserves?
Does an objective marker that has been Sabotaged by my army using the A Deadly Prize Stratagem remain Sabotaged if my opponent controls it at the start or end of any turn?
answer: Yes. However, while your opponent controls that objective marker, its ability to inflict mortal wounds is not active.

question: While an Attached unit is embarked within a **TRANSPORT**, do weapons that **TRANSPORT** shoots with using the Firing Deck ability benefit from any abilities the attached Leader has (e.g. a Lieutenant’s Tactical Precision ability, which grants weapons in its unit the **[LETHAL HITS]** ability)?
answer: No. Firing Deck enables a **TRANSPORT** to be equipped with a number of weapons equipped by its passengers (Core Rules), but the **TRANSPORT** itself is not part of that Attached unit.

question: Can a unit embarked within a **TRANSPORT** be selected as the Oath of Moment target?
Can a unit in Reserves be selected as the Oath of Moment target?
Can a unit in Reserves use an Enhancement (following all restrictions outlined by that Enhancement)?
If I have two or more Impulsors within 6" of a unit I target with a Stratagem, do each of their Orbital Comms Array wargear abilities trigger?
answer: No, they are Aura abilities and so do not stack.

question: If I use a Stratagem on a unit within 6" of an Impulsor with an Orbital Comms Array, but I do not roll high enough to gain 1CP as a result of that wargear, if that same unit is within 6" of a different Impulsor that also has an Orbital Comms Array, can I attempt to roll another D6 to try to gain 1CP?
Do multiple instances of the Astartes Banner ability stack?

If I select one **MONSTER** or **VEHICLE** unit as the target of all of a Stormspeeder Thunderstrike’s ranged weapons, and the first weapon that model makes attacks with scores one or more hits, do I add 1 to the Wound rolls made for attacks with its remaining weapons?
answer: No. Only after you have resolved the attacks with all weapons it is shooting with this phase, has that model shot (see ‘Shot’ in the Rules Appendix, 04.03.04).

question: When making attacks with a unit from my army, I split my attacks between the first Oath of Moment target selected as part of my army rule and the second Oath of Moment target selected using Roboute Guilliman’s Master of Battle Author of the Codex ability. If I destroy the first Oath of Moment target before resolving my attacks against the second Oath of Moment target, will those attacks against the second Oath of Moment target benefit from the re-rolls to hit?
answer: No. Only after all of the attacks from that unit have been resolved will the second Oath of Moment selection become active.
